p1_candidate_matrix.rationale: Vocabulary gap (block/era/year/robustness) is purely definitional — no DB access, no new statistical logic required. P252F rolling_window.py already provides the rolling slice foundation. M7 adds: (a) stability vocabulary constants, (b) block-split helper, (c) threshold SSOT. Type C additive — small, low-risk, no edge-claim risk. rolling_window.py + stability_diagnostics.py form a natural pair.
p1_candidate_matrix.db_schema_risk: NONE — pure Python, no DB access
p1_candidate_matrix.expected_value: Closes vocabulary inconsistency; enables consistent cross-script stability reporting
scope: block_stability() helper — split hits into non-overlapping blocks

p1_candidate_matrix.rationale: Positional data for 3_STAR/4_STAR is now in the DB (P213H/L — 5,850 rows each with numbers_positional). The gap is a unified SSOT parser module, not data recovery. BIG_LOTTO/DAILY_539/POWER_LOTTO use ad-hoc upload scripts without a shared schema contract. Before implementing a parser SSOT, a read-only inventory of all per-lottery parsing scripts is needed to define the shared input/output schema. Type B design task needed first.
p1_candidate_matrix.implementation_risk: MEDIUM — parser SSOT touches data ingestion path
p1_candidate_matrix.db_schema_risk: LOW for read-only inventory; MEDIUM for actual SSOT requiring validation
p1_candidate_matrix.expected_value: Unified parse contract prevents silent data drift on new draw ingestion
p1_candidate_matrix.estimated_type: Type B (inventory) → Type C (implementation, separate task)
blocking_issues: No inventory of per-lottery format contracts yet
Source format for 3_STAR/4_STAR NEW draws unconfirmed (P213G validation was mock-only)
scope: Read-only inventory of existing per-lottery parse scripts
Define shared parser SSOT input schema (per-lottery)

p1_candidate_matrix.rationale: No unified feature definition exists for this lottery system. 'Feature' in the bottleneck report context (mutual information per feature vs draw outcome) requires a pre-defined feature vocabulary that this project does not have as a SSOT. P219 diagnostic sweep has partial MI-style analysis but it is ad-hoc. statistical_diagnostics_schema.py has 'feature_bottleneck' as a REQUIRED_SCHEMA_FIELD but no implementation. M8 cannot be usefully implemented until: (a) a feature vocabulary is defined (which requires a separate design task), (b) the null baseline for MI under random draws is established. Risk: overclaiming features as 'predictive' when MI under random null is high. Recommend deferring until after M7 is complete and a separate feature design task is authorized.
p1_candidate_matrix.implementation_risk: HIGH — overclaim risk without feature vocabulary + null baseline
p1_candidate_matrix.db_schema_risk: NONE for design; MEDIUM for MI computation (needs feature vectors)
p1_candidate_matrix.edge_claim_risk: HIGH — mutual information can be misinterpreted as predictive signal; requires careful null comparison
p1_candidate_matrix.expected_value: Interpretability only; no prediction edge expected
p1_candidate_matrix.estimated_type: Type B (feature design) → Type C (implementation) — multi-step
blocking_issues: MI null baseline under random draws not established
Overclaim risk without proper null comparison framework

readiness_decision.rationale: M7 is the most natural next P1 step: it directly extends P252F (rolling_window.py) already on main; Type C low-risk implementation; pure vocabulary/constants + block-split helper; no DB access; no edge-claim risk; closes the inconsistent block/era/year/robustness label gap. M1 needs a read-only inventory first (Type B preceding Type C). M8 is deferred due to missing feature vocabulary and overclaim risk.

recommended_next_task.title: Implement M7 Signal Stability Diagnostics SSOT
scope: STABILITY_VOCAB: BLOCK = ERA = YEAR (synonyms); ROBUSTNESS = subset-exclusion check
Threshold constants: STABLE / WARNING / UNSTABLE (aligned with DriftDetector PSI)
block_stability(hits, block_size) — split hit list into non-overlapping blocks, return per-block stats
stability_summary(hits, block_sizes, label) — structured output with no_edge_claim=True
Extends rolling_window.py (P252F) — can import rolling_slices internally
non_scope: Do not change DriftDetector or RSM production code
Do not claim stability implies prediction edge
Do not modify existing completed-task scripts
recommended_next_task.authorization_required: Explicit 'Authorize P253B M7 signal stability diagnostics SSOT'
recommended_next_task.estimated_effort: Similar to P252C-F (1 module + analysis script + tests)

rejected_or_deferred_options.reason: Type B design inventory needed before Type C implementation
rejected_or_deferred_options.unblock_condition: Complete a read-only parser script inventory and define input/output schema

rejected_or_deferred_options.reason: Missing feature vocabulary + high overclaim risk without null MI baseline
rejected_or_deferred_options.unblock_condition: Define feature vocabulary SSOT + establish MI null distribution under random draws. Authorize separately after M7 is complete.

final_decision: P253A triage complete. M7 Signal Stability Diagnostics is the recommended next P1 task. M7 readiness: READY_FOR_NEXT_TASK — extends P252F rolling_window.py, Type C, no DB, no edge-claim risk. M1 readiness: NEEDS_READONLY_INVENTORY — parser script survey needed before implementation. M8 readiness: DEFER — missing feature vocabulary + overclaim risk. No deployable prediction edge. No betting advice. No strategy promotion. System remains WAITING_FOR_USER_AUTHORIZATION. Authorization phrase for next: 'Authorize P253B M7 signal stability diagnostics SSOT'.
